sections.paragraph:
ἀχρειόω (s. ἀχρεῖος) (t.r., S., Vog.; by-form ἀχρεόω Tdf., W-H., M., Bov., N25; SIG 569, 31; s. B-D-F §30, 2) 1 aor. inf. ἀχρειῶσαι LXX; 3 pl. pf. ἠχρείωκαν Da 6:21. Pass. 1 aor. ἠχρεώθην; pf. ptc. ἠχρειωμένος (Philo Mech. 60, 16; Polyb. 3, 64, 8 al.; Vett. Val. 290, 1; OGI 573, 16: LXX; ἠχρειώθησαν Just., D. 27, 3; Theoph. Ant. 2, 35 [p. 188, 29]) in our lit. only pass.

① make useless, outwardly, in symbolism, of damaged sticks Hs 8, 3, 4.

② of becoming a liability to society because of moral depravity become depraved, worthless of pers. Ro 3:12 (Ps 13:3; 52:4).—M-M.

t.r. t.r. = textus receptus

Vog. Vog. = HVogels, editor of the Greek NT—List 1, beg.

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

Bov Bov = JBover, ed. of the Greek NT—List 1, beg.

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

  1. pl. = plural
  2. pf. = perfect

ptc. ptc. = participle

Philo Philo Mech , III–II b.c.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

OGI OGI = Orientis Graeci Inscriptiones Selectae—List 3

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

Hs Hs = Similitudes

pers. pers. = person(s)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 160
